---
title: "Utilization-Focused Evaluation Overview"
description: "Study note on Utilization-Focused Evaluation Overview in Essentials of Utilization-Focused Evaluation."
book_id: "essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "27-28"
citation_key: "patton_2012_essentials_utilization_focused_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:ch-introduction-overview-and-context:c0007"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2012</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Source pages 27-28</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "Utilization-Focused Evaluation Overview" from Michael Quinn Patton's *Essentials of Utilization-Focused Evaluation* [@patton_2012_essentials_utilization_focused_evaluation]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

The overview anchors U-FE in a practical sequence from findings to interpretation to action.

## Study Summary

The overview anchors U-FE in a practical sequence from findings to interpretation to action.

Reports are not the purpose of evaluation; use is the purpose.

The evaluator facilitates a process in which design decisions are judged by utility and actual use.

## Key Concepts

- Utility
- Actual use
- Primary intended users
- Action

## Source Location

- Section path: Introduction, Overview, and Context
- Evidence records: `chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:ch-introduction-overview-and-context:c0007`

## Practical Implications

- Name who will use the evaluation and how.
- Make use criteria visible in design decisions.
- Plan beyond the report to interpretation and action.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that Utilization-Focused Evaluation Overview helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## References
